name: CalFresh (SNAP) — Student Exemption
description: Federal food assistance of ~$292/month. Most students with Pell Grant qualify under the student exemption — but most never apply because they assume students don't qualify.
how_to_access: Apply at benefitscal.com. Takes 20-30 minutes. Bring your student ID and Pell Grant award letter. Benefits load onto an EBT card within 30 days, often sooner.

name: California Food Assistance Program (CFAP)
description: California-only food benefits for immigrants who don't qualify for federal CalFresh. As of April 2026, DACA and some visa holders are excluded from federal CalFresh but may qualify here.
how_to_access: Apply through your county social services office. Berkeley/Oakland: Alameda County DHCS at 1111 Jackson St, Oakland.

name: UC Berkeley Food Pantry — MLK Student Union
description: Free groceries in the lower level of MLK Student Union — literally one floor below the hackathon venue. No documentation required, no income verification.
how_to_access: Walk in during open hours. Bring your Cal 1 Card. Friday afternoons have the shortest lines. You can visit once per week.

name: Grab N Go Recovered Meals
description: Free daily meals recovered from campus dining. Available at the Basic Needs Center pickup points. No signup, no questions.
how_to_access: Check the Basic Needs Center daily posting on Instagram (@ucb_basicneeds) for today's pickup location and time. Usually weekday afternoons.

name: Emergency Meal Swipes Program
description: Up to 14 free swipes for campus dining halls, available to any student without an active meal plan.
how_to_access: Email basicneeds@berkeley.edu or stop by University Hall. Usually approved same-day. Works at all campus dining halls.

name: Market Match — Double Your CalFresh at Farmers Market
description: At the Saturday Berkeley Farmers Market, Market Match doubles your first $10 of CalFresh spending on fruits and vegetables. Effectively free produce.
how_to_access: Bring your EBT card to the info booth at the Saturday Berkeley Farmers Market (Center St & MLK Way). Ask for Market Match tokens.

name: Berkeley Free Food Calendar — Club Events
description: Dozens of student clubs host events with free catering every week — info sessions, networking nights, cultural celebrations. Most students don't think to attend for the food.
how_to_access: Check the Berkeley Events calendar at events.berkeley.edu and filter for events with food. Also check your department's email list and department Discord.

name: Berkeley Student Food Collective
description: Student-run grocery store with sliding-scale pricing. You pay what you can. Located on campus.
how_to_access: Located in the lower level of Hearst Gym. Walk in during open hours (Mon-Fri). Tell them your situation — they will work with you on price.
dollar_value: Sliding scale — pay what you can

name: Basic Needs Center Emergency Housing
description: Short-term emergency housing for Berkeley students facing homelessness or housing insecurity. Connects to longer-term options. Available to all students regardless of documentation.
how_to_access: Call (510) 642-6325 or walk into University Hall 201 during business hours. If after hours, call the 24/7 emergency line. Don't wait — contact them today.

name: BSC Co-ops — 30–50% Cheaper Than Dorms
description: The Berkeley Student Cooperative runs 17 co-op houses near campus. Room and board runs $1,000–$1,400/month vs $2,200+ for dorms. Rolling admissions — most students never apply because they don't know BSC exists.
how_to_access: Apply at bsc.coop/apply. Applications are rolling — apply anytime. Financial aid can be applied to co-op costs. Call (510) 848-1936 for current availability.

name: Berkeley Rent Control — Know Your Rights
description: Apartments built before 1980 in Berkeley have legally capped annual rent increases (currently ~2%). If your landlord raised rent more than that, you may be owed money back.
how_to_access: Look up your address at rent.berkeleyca.gov. If your building is covered, contact the Berkeley Rent Board at (510) 981-7368 for a free consultation.
effort_level: Address lookup (2 min), then free consult

name: FAFSA Cost-of-Attendance Housing Appeal
description: Berkeley's financial aid office uses a standard housing cost estimate. If your actual off-campus rent exceeds that estimate, you can appeal for more aid — potentially thousands more.
how_to_access: Contact the Financial Aid Office at financialaid.berkeley.edu or (510) 642-0485. Request a 'Professional Judgment' appeal for housing costs. Bring 3 months of rent receipts.

name: Lease Red-Flag Scanner
description: Paste your lease text here and Jugaad will identify predatory clauses — illegal fees, improper security deposit terms, waived habitability rights, and more.
how_to_access: Use the Jugaad lease scanner feature. Paste your lease text. Takes 30 seconds.

name: Alameda County Rapid Rehousing
description: Short-term rental assistance and housing placement for students experiencing homelessness in Alameda County. Available regardless of immigration status.
how_to_access: Get a referral through the Basic Needs Center at basicneeds.berkeley.edu. They'll connect you to the county program. Do not apply directly — go through Basic Needs first.

name: Special Circumstances Appeal — Recalculate Your Aid
description: Your FAFSA uses last year's income. If your family's financial situation changed (job loss, divorce, medical bills), the Financial Aid Office can recalculate your aid using current income — which can add thousands of dollars.
how_to_access: Contact the Financial Aid Office at Sproul Hall Room 200 or (510) 642-0485. Ask for a 'Special Circumstances Appeal.' Bring documentation of the income change (termination letter, tax return, medical bills).

name: UC Berkeley Emergency Grant
description: One-time emergency grants of $500–$1,000 for unexpected financial hardship. Rolling applications, no repayment required. Administered by the Financial Aid Office.
how_to_access: Apply online at financialaid.berkeley.edu/emergency-funding or walk into Sproul Hall Room 200. You'll need a brief personal statement describing the emergency. Jugaad can generate this for you.

name: Short-Term Emergency Loan (FAFSA Bridge)
description: Interest-free loans of up to $1,500 to bridge the gap while waiting for financial aid disbursement — especially critical now that FAFSA processing is paused post-April 21, 2026.
how_to_access: Apply at the Financial Aid Office, Sproul Hall Room 200. Repaid automatically when your financial aid disburses. Usually approved within 2-3 business days.

name: Micro-Scholarship Scan — Departmental Awards
description: Hundreds of $500–$2,000 departmental scholarships go unclaimed every semester because students don't know they exist. Your department almost certainly has at least 3.
how_to_access: Email your department's undergraduate advisor and ask: 'Are there any scholarships or awards available for students in [your major]?' This one email has gotten students thousands of dollars.

name: Tuition Fee Payment Plan
description: Split tuition into 3 installments across the semester without late fees or interest. Available to all enrolled students.
how_to_access: Log into CalCentral > My Finances > Billing > Payment Plan. Set up online in 5 minutes before the payment deadline.

name: California Dream Act — AB 540 Financial Aid
description: If you attended a California high school for 3+ years, you qualify for the Dream Act regardless of immigration status. This gives access to Cal Grant B (up to $13K/year) and many institutional scholarships.
how_to_access: Complete the Dream Act application at dream.csac.ca.gov instead of FAFSA. Deadline: March 2. Contact the Undocumented Student Program at (510) 642-7294 for help.

name: Pell Grant Appeal — Maximum Award
description: Students with SAI/EFC of $0 are eligible for maximum Pell Grant ($7,395/year in 2025-26). If your award is less, you may be able to appeal or it may update automatically when you complete FAFSA correctly.
how_to_access: Contact the Financial Aid Office to verify your Pell Grant amount. If your SAI is $0 and you're receiving less than the maximum, request a review.

name: NightSafe — Free Late-Night Shuttle
description: Free late-night shuttle service around Berkeley, running until 2am. Way safer and faster than walking alone at night.
how_to_access: Download the Transloc app or visit ncts.berkeley.edu to see real-time shuttle locations and routes. Runs 7pm–2am daily.

name: SafeWalk — UCPD Escort Service
description: A UCPD officer will walk with you anywhere on or near campus, 24/7. No questions asked. Massively underutilized.
how_to_access: Call UCPD non-emergency at (510) 642-6760 and ask for SafeWalk. They'll meet you wherever you are within a few minutes.

name: Safe Route: Oxford Street at Night
description: Oxford Street (west side of campus) is better lit and has lower incident rates than Telegraph Ave for late-night travel to Southside and south Berkeley.
how_to_access: When heading south from campus after dark, take Oxford St instead of Telegraph Ave. 3 minutes longer, significantly safer.

name: BearWATCH — Anonymous Safety Tip Line
description: Report safety concerns or suspicious activity anonymously. Text-based, confidential.
how_to_access: Text 'TIP UCPD' followed by your message to 888777. You can also submit tips at ucpd.berkeley.edu/report.

name: Let's Talk — Free Drop-In Counseling (No Appointment)
description: Free informal counseling with a real CAPS counselor — no appointment, no waitlist, no paperwork. Multiple campus locations. Most students don't know this exists and wait weeks for a CAPS appointment instead.
how_to_access: Check the Let's Talk schedule at uhs.berkeley.edu/counseling/lets-talk for this week's locations and times. Just show up. Usually 15-30 min per person.

name: SHIP Off-Campus Therapist — Same-Week Availability
description: Your student health insurance (SHIP) covers off-campus licensed therapists. These providers often have same-week or next-week availability while CAPS has a weeks-long waitlist. Most students don't know they can go off-campus.
how_to_access: Go to the Optum/UHC provider portal (your SHIP carrier) and search for in-network therapists in Berkeley. Filter for 'accepting new patients.' Call directly — don't go through CAPS.

name: CAPS Urgent Appointment Pathway
description: CAPS reserves same-day urgent slots that most students don't know about. If you tell them you're in distress and need to be seen today, they will find a slot. Students who don't communicate urgency wait 3-4 weeks.
how_to_access: Call CAPS at (510) 642-9494. When they answer, say: 'I'm a student in distress and I need to be seen today or this week as an urgent appointment.' Do not just ask for 'the next available.'
effort_level: Phone call — be specific about urgency

name: 24/7 Counseling Line — Call Anytime
description: A real counselor available any time, day or night. Not a crisis hotline — actual counseling support.
how_to_access: Call 855-817-5667. Available 24/7, 365 days a year. Free for UC Berkeley students.

name: Peer Wellness Coaching — Tang Center
description: Free 1-on-1 sessions with trained peer wellness coaches. Covers stress, sleep, anxiety, relationships, and academic pressure. Often next-day availability.
how_to_access: Book at uhs.berkeley.edu/wellness-coaching. Available in-person at Tang Center and via Zoom.

name: BerkeleyTime — Check Grade Distributions Before Enrolling
description: BerkeleyTime shows historical grade distributions for every course. CS 10 had a 35.3% F rate this semester — up from under 10% historically. This data is public but most students don't look at it before enrolling.
how_to_access: Go to berkeleytime.com, search your course. Click 'Grades' to see historical distributions by semester and professor. Choose sections with instructors who have better grade distributions for the same course.

name: Phase 1 Enrollment Strategy — Get Into Impacted Courses
description: Most students don't know the optimal strategy for Phase 1 enrollment. Adding a course to your waitlist at Phase 1 gives you waitlist priority. Specific unit loading strategies also affect your appointment time.
how_to_access: In Phase 1: prioritize impacted required courses over electives. In Phase 2: add remaining courses. For waitlists: get on them in Phase 1, even if you're #20 — many spots open after add/drop.

name: Fee Deferral — Remove Registration Holds
description: Unpaid tuition generates a registration hold that blocks enrollment. The fee deferral splits payment into installments and removes the hold immediately — you don't have to pay anything upfront.
how_to_access: Log into CalCentral > My Finances > Billing > Payment Plan. Set up the deferral before the enrollment deadline. Hold is lifted within 24 hours.

name: Free Department Tutoring — CSC, ASUC
description: Most departments offer free tutoring that goes almost completely unused. CS tutoring at the Student Learning Center is staffed by graduate students and is often better than office hours.
how_to_access: For CS: visit slc.berkeley.edu for the Computer Science Tutoring schedule. For other departments: email your department's undergraduate advisor and ask 'what tutoring resources are available?'

name: Study Group Matching via Course Discords
description: Every large Berkeley course has an unofficial Discord server where students organize study groups. These study groups dramatically improve performance and most students don't know they exist.
how_to_access: Search '[course name] Berkeley Discord' on Google. For CS courses, also check the CSUA (Computer Science Undergrad Association) Discord. Join the semester channel and introduce yourself.
